creator: a@jenisch.at
text:
I'm trying to install FreeBSD9 (64bit) on a HP Proliant server 
(ProLiant DL385 G1). Installation is done via remote-management
("iLO") basically by mounting the Installation-ISO.

However a couple of seconds after booting the box crashes.

Here's what I tried already:

o) Re-download the installation ISO - same checksum - same result

o) Instead of booting directly I issued the following two commands 
on the loader prompt:

set kern.eventtimer.periodic=1
set kern.eventtimer.timer="LAPIC"

followed by "boot"

Neither of these helped.

To cross-check I tried booting of a FreeBSD 8 installation media - no
problems at all (besides that the machine is running FreeBSD8
currently without problems)


I've uploaded screenshots of the aborting install-session under

http://www.jenisch.at/FreeBSD-9-install-fails-on-HP-proliant/FreeBSD-9-crash-while-installing-on-HP-DL385-G1-screenshot-01.jpg

http://www.jenisch.at/FreeBSD-9-install-fails-on-HP-proliant/FreeBSD-9-crash-while-installing-on-HP-DL385-G1-screenshot-04.jpg

http://www.jenisch.at/FreeBSD-9-install-fails-on-HP-proliant/FreeBSD-9-crash-while-installing-on-HP-DL385-G1-screenshot-07.jpg

http://www.jenisch.at/FreeBSD-9-install-fails-on-HP-proliant/FreeBSD-9-crash-while-installing-on-HP-DL385-G1-screenshot-09.jpg

showing the different stages. In the end the box goes south completely so that the only way to get it back to life again is a full power cycle :-(.

Thanks in advance for any clue,
-ewald

How-To-Repeat: 100% reproducible
